subject: Sociology
title: Global Migration Patterns
description: Learn Global Migration Patterns in one minute.

hook.text: 281 million people live outside their birth country. That's more than the entire population of most nations.

buildup.text: Migration is driven by push factors like poverty and conflict, and pull factors like jobs and safety.

discovery.text: Most migration is regional, not intercontinental. Africans move within Africa; Asians move within Asia.

twist.text: Migrants send $600 billion home yearly in remittances — more than all foreign aid combined. Migration builds economies.

climax.text: Migration is humanity's oldest response to hardship. Borders are new; human movement is not.

punchline.text: People have always moved toward hope.

quiz.question: What are 'push factors' in migration?
options: Reasons people are attracted to a destination
Conditions like poverty and conflict that drive people to leave
